title: Get a police accident report
description: Get a copy of the police report for a road traffic accident.
summary: If you were involved in a road traffic accident in Barbados, you can ask for a copy of the police report. Insurance companies almost always need one before they will settle a claim.

The form asks for the date, time and place of the accident, the registration numbers of the vehicles involved, and what you need the report for. Anyone with a legitimate interest – the driver, the owner, the named insured, an insurance company or an attorney – can apply.
The fee is $50 BBD. Pay it at the Barbados Revenue Authority and bring the receipt with you when you submit the form.
Reports can take a few weeks to prepare, especially for serious accidents that are still being investigated. Plan around that timeline if you have a deadline from your insurer.
who_can_apply: the driver, owner or named insured of a vehicle involved in the accident
an insurance company or attorney acting for someone involved

items: your name, address, phone number and email
the date and time of the accident
the registration numbers of the vehicles involved
your reason for asking for the report

items.name: Proof of relationship
items.note: For example, the registration card if you are the owner, or a letter of authority if you are acting for someone else.

items.name: Receipt of payment of the fee

notes: Pay at the Barbados Revenue Authority before you apply.

how_long: Reports are usually ready within 4 to 6 weeks.

options.heading: In person
content: Hand the completed Application for Police Accident Report form in to Police Headquarters, James Street, St. Michael.

contact.address:
Police Headquarters
Roebuck Street
St. Michael

assumptions: The $50 BBD fee is from the form. ‘Pay it at the Barbados Revenue Authority’ is inferred from how Police fees are normally collected.
‘Reports are usually ready within 4 to 6 weeks’ is an estimate.
Document list (photo ID, proof of relationship, receipt) is inferred from common practice.
